TASK: We discovered our data is stored in US-East region but our contract (section 12.3) specifies EU-only data residency. Investigation reveals: (1) When our account was provisioned 18 months ago, EU residency was correctly set. But 4 months ago, after your 'infrastructure modernization' announcement, we noticed our dashboard's latency improved significantly — suspicious given our users are all in Frankfurt, (2) A traceroute from our Frankfurt office to your API endpoint now resolves to US-East-1 IP ranges (previously resolved to eu-central-1), (3) We tested data residency using your admin panel's 'data location' feature — it shows 'EU (Ireland)' but the network evidence contradicts this, (4) If our data has been in the US for 4 months, this is a GDPR Article 44 violation — unauthorized international transfer without SCCs, binding corporate rules, or adequacy decision, (5) Our DPO must report this to the Hessian DPA within 72 hours of confirmation, which means we need a definitive answer within 24 hours: is our data currently in the EU or not? If it was moved without authorization, when, why, and what safeguards were applied?, (6) Depending on the answer, we may need to invoke the emergency data portability clause in our contract.
